text: {"id": "EMG_HEP_001", "disease_name": "Acute Liver Failure", "category": "Liver", "severity": "Highly Critical", "description": "Rapid loss of liver function occurring over days to weeks in a person without prior liver disease, often due to toxins, drugs, or viral hepatitis.", "key_symptoms": ["jaundice", "upper right abdominal pain", "nausea and vomiting", "fatigue", "confusion", "sleepiness", "abdominal swelling"], "red_flags": ["altered mental status", "severe confusion", "bleeding tendency", "seizures", "coma", "rapid jaundice progression"], "common_causes": ["acetaminophen overdose", "viral hepatitis", "drug induced liver injury", "toxin exposure", "autoimmune hepatitis", "metabolic disorders", "sepsis or shock"], "risk_factors": ["acetaminophen overuse", "viral hepatitis infection", "female sex", "use of hepatotoxic drugs", "autoimmune disease", "toxin exposure", "pregnancy related liver disease"], "triage_priority": "Very High Risk (within 10-30 Minutes)", "actions": ["assess airway breathing circulation", "check mental status for encephalopathy", "obtain urgent liver function tests", "monitor coagulation profile", "prepare for ICU admission", "evaluate for liver transplant"], "complications": ["cerebral edema", "severe bleeding", "infection", "kidney failure", "coma", "death"], "keywords": ["acute liver failure", "fulminant hepatic failure", "sudden jaundice confusion", "rapid liver failure", "toxic liver injury"]}

{"id": "EMG_TOX_001", "disease_name": "Alcohol Poisoning", "category": "Toxicological", "severity": "Critical", "description": "Alcohol poisoning is a life-threatening condition caused by rapid or excessive intake of ethanol or other toxic alcohols, leading to central nervous system and respiratory depression.", "key_symptoms": ["confusion", "vomiting", "seizures", "slow breathing", "irregular breathing", "blue or pale skin", "low body temperature", "difficulty staying conscious"], "red_flags": ["unconsciousness", "breathing fewer than eight breaths per minute", "gaps greater than 10 seconds between breaths", "repeated vomiting with reduced consciousness", "seizures after alcohol intake", "suspected ingestion of toxic alcohol"], "common_causes": ["binge drinking", "rapid consumption of large alcohol amounts", "ingestion of mouthwash or household alcohol products", "isopropyl alcohol ingestion", "methanol ingestion", "ethylene glycol ingestion"], "risk_factors": ["binge drinking behavior", "low body weight", "drinking on an empty stomach", "concurrent drug use", "use of alcohol-containing household products", "young age or inexperience with alcohol", "medical conditions affecting alcohol metabolism"], "triage_priority": "Urgent (Within 1 Hour)", "actions": ["call emergency services immediately", "assess airway, breathing, and circulation", "place patient in recovery position if unconscious", "monitor respiratory rate and oxygen saturation", "administer oxygen if needed", "prepare for advanced airway management", "transport urgently to emergency department"], "complications": ["respiratory failure", "aspiration and choking", "severe dehydration", "seizures", "hypothermia", "cardiac arrest", "brain damage", "death"], "noise_keywords_to_ignore": ["mild intoxication", "social
drinking", "hangover symptoms", "remote past alcohol use"], "source": "Mayo Clinic"}

{"id": "IMM_ANAPH_004", "disease_name": "Anaphylaxis", "category": "Respiratory & Allergic Emergency", "severity": "Fatal", "description": "Anaphylaxis is a severe, life-threatening allergic reaction that occurs rapidly after exposure to an allergen and can cause airway obstruction and shock.", "key_symptoms": ["difficulty breathing", "airway swelling", "swollen throat", "wheezing", "low blood pressure", "rapid weak pulse", "loss of consciousness"], "other_symptoms": ["hives", "itching", "flushed skin", "pale skin", "nausea", "vomiting", "diarrhea", "dizziness", "fainting"], "red_flags": ["sudden breathing difficulty after allergen exposure", "throat or tongue swelling", "signs of shock with allergic reaction", "rapid drop in blood pressure", "collapse after exposure to allergen"], "risk_factors": ["previous anaphylaxis", "food allergies", "drug allergies", "insect sting allergy", "latex allergy", "asthma", "mastocytosis", "heart disease"], "actions": ["inject epinephrine immediately if available", "call emergency services immediately", "lay the person flat and elevate legs if possible", "avoid giving food or drink", "repeat epinephrine if symptoms persist and trained to do so"], "triage_priority": "Immediate", "common_tests": ["clinical diagnosis", "serum tryptase test", "allergy testing"], "source": "Mayo Clinic"}

{"id": "IMM_ASTH_005", "name": "Asthma Attack", "category": "Respiratory", "severity": "Serious", "description": "A sudden worsening of asthma symptoms caused by airway narrowing, inflammation, and mucus buildup leading to breathing difficulty.", "key_symptoms": ["Shortness of breath", "Chest tightness or pain", "Coughing", "Wheezing"], "red_flags": ["Gasping for breath", "Difficulty speaking due to breathlessness", "Severe chest muscle strain while breathing", "Peak flow below 50% of personal best", "Symptoms not improving after rescue inhaler"], "common_causes": ["Allergic reactions to pollen, dust mites, mold, pets", "Respiratory infections such as cold or flu", "Tobacco smoke exposure", "Cold dry air", "Exercise", "Air pollution or chemical irritants", "Certain medications such as aspirin or NSAIDs", "Gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD)"], "risk_factors": ["Poorly controlled asthma", "Exposure to environmental triggers", "Not taking daily controller medication", "Incorrect inhaler technique", "Depression or anxiety", "Coexisting chronic illnesses"], "triage_priority": "Very High Risk (within 10-30 Minutes)", "actions": ["Use quick-relief inhaler as prescribed", "Follow asthma action plan zones", "Check peak flow if available", "Seek emergency care if severe symptoms occur", "Continue prescribed controller medications"], "complications": ["Frequent emergency visits", "Interrupted sleep", "Limited physical activity", "Missed work or school", "Life-threatening respiratory failure"], "keywords": ["asthma exacerbation", "asthma flare", "wheezing attack", "breathing difficulty asthma", "acute asthma"]}

{"id": "CARD_SCA_001", "disease_name": "Sudden Cardiac Arrest", "category": "Cardiovascular", "severity": "Fatal", "description": "Sudden cardiac arrest is the sudden loss of heart activity due to an abnormal heart rhythm, causing breathing to stop and loss of consciousness.", "key_symptoms": ["sudden collapse", "no pulse", "no breathing", "loss of consciousness"], "other_symptoms": ["chest discomfort", "shortness of breath", "weakness", "palpitations", "rapid heartbeat", "irregular heartbeat"], "red_flags": ["person is unconscious and not breathing", "absence of pulse", "sudden collapse with unresponsiveness"], "risk_factors": ["coronary artery disease", "previous heart attack", "cardiomyopathy", "heart valve disease", "congenital heart defect", "long QT syndrome", "family history of heart disease", "smoking", "high blood pressure", "high cholesterol", "obesity", "diabetes", "inactive lifestyle", "illicit drug use", "low potassium", "low magnesium", "obstructive sleep apnea", "chronic kidney disease", "older age", "male sex"], "actions": ["call emergency services immediately", "start CPR with hard and fast chest compressions", "use an automated external defibrillator if available", "continue CPR until emergency help arrives"], "triage_priority": "Immediate", "common_tests": ["electrocardiogram", "cardiac monitoring", "blood tests", "coronary angiography"], "source": "Mayo Clinic"}

{"id": "EMG_END_001", "disease_name": "Diabetic Ketoacidosis", "category": "Endocrine", "severity": "Critical", "description": "Diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA) is a life-threatening complication of diabetes caused by severe insulin deficiency leading to hyperglycemia, ketosis, and metabolic acidosis.", "key_symptoms": ["excessive thirst", "frequent urination", "nausea", "vomiting", "abdominal pain", "fatigue", "shortness of breath", "fruity breath odor", "confusion"], "red_flags": ["blood glucose above 300 mg/dL", "moderate or high urine ketones", "persistent vomiting", "deep or labored breathing", "altered mental status", "signs of dehydration", "loss of consciousness"], "common_causes": ["insulin deficiency", "missed insulin doses", "infection", "acute illness"], "risk_factors": ["type 1 diabetes", "poor insulin adherence", "insulin pump failure", "recent infection", "myocardial infarction", "stroke", "pancreatitis", "pregnancy", "corticosteroid use", "cocaine use"], "triage_priority": "Very High Risk (within 10-30 Minutes)", "actions": ["check capillary blood glucose", "test blood or urine ketones", "assess hydration and mental status", "establish IV access", "begin isotonic fluid resuscitation", "initiate insulin therapy per protocol", "monitor electrolytes especially potassium", "treat underlying trigger such as infection", "urgent hospital admission"], "complications": ["severe dehydration", "hypokalemia", "hypoglycemia during treatment", "cerebral edema", "coma", "death"], "noise_keywords_to_ignore": ["well controlled diabetes without symptoms", "isolated mild hyperglycemia", "trace urine ketones without symptoms"], "source": "Mayo Clinic"}

{"id": "EMG_OBS_001", "disease_name": "Ectopic Pregnancy", "category": "Reproductive", "severity": "Highly Critical", "description": "Ectopic pregnancy occurs when a fertilized egg implants outside the uterus, most commonly in a fallopian tube, and can lead to life-threatening internal bleeding if rupture occurs.", "key_symptoms": ["pelvic pain", "light vaginal bleeding", "missed period", "breast tenderness", "nausea", "shoulder pain", "urge to have bowel movement"], "red_flags": ["severe abdominal or pelvic pain with vaginal bleeding", "fainting", "extreme lightheadedness", "signs of shock", "suspected tubal rupture"], "common_causes": ["damaged fallopian tube", "tubal inflammation", "hormonal imbalance", "abnormal fertilized egg development"], "risk_factors": ["previous ectopic pregnancy", "sexually transmitted infections", "pelvic inflammatory disease", "fertility treatments such as IVF", "tubal surgery", "pregnancy with IUD in place", "tubal ligation history", "cigarette smoking"], "triage_priority": "Very High Risk (within 10-30 Minutes)", "actions": ["perform urgent pregnancy test", "obtain transvaginal ultrasound", "assess hemodynamic stability", "establish IV access", "prepare for emergency surgical evaluation if unstable", "monitor for signs of internal bleeding", "urgent obstetric/gynecologic consultation"], "complications": ["fallopian tube rupture", "severe internal bleeding", "hemorrhagic shock", "infertility", "maternal death"], "noise_keywords_to_ignore": ["normal early pregnancy symptoms only", "mild menstrual cramps", "remote history of ectopic pregnancy without current symptoms"], "source": "Mayo Clinic"}

{"id": "EMG_NEU_002", "disease_name": "Encephalitis", "category": "Neurological", "severity": "Critical", "description": "Encephalitis is inflammation of the brain most commonly caused by viral infection or autoimmune processes, leading to neurological dysfunction that can be life-threatening.", "key_symptoms": ["fever", "headache", "confusion", "personality changes", "seizures", "stiff neck", "muscle weakness", "irregular movements", "trouble speaking", "loss of consciousness"], "red_flags": ["altered mental status", "new onset seizures", "loss of consciousness", "severe headache with fever", "neurological deficits", "coma", "rapid neurological deterioration"], "common_causes": ["viral infections", "herpes simplex virus", "mosquito borne viruses", "tick borne viruses", "autoimmune reaction"], "risk_factors": ["young children", "older adults", "weakened immune system", "HIV infection", "immunosuppressive therapy", "autoimmune disease", "exposure to mosquitoes or ticks", "smoking associated malignancy"], "triage_priority": "Urgent (Within 1 Hour)", "actions": ["assess airway breathing circulation", "check vital signs and neurological status", "obtain urgent neuroimaging", "perform lumbar puncture if indicated", "start empiric antiviral therapy", "manage seizures if present", "admit to hospital urgently", "consult neurology and infectious disease"], "complications": ["brain damage", "persistent neurological deficits", "memory impairment", "personality changes", "coma", "death"], "noise_keywords_to_ignore": ["simple viral fever without neurological signs", "isolated mild headache", "chronic stable neurological symptoms"], "source": "Mayo Clinic"}

{"id": "NEU_EPILEP_005", "disease_name": "Epilepsy (Seizure Disorder)", "category": "Neurological", "severity": "Serious", "description": "Epilepsy is a neurological condition characterized by recurrent unprovoked seizures caused by abnormal electrical activity in the brain.", "key_symptoms": ["recurrent seizures", "convulsions", "jerking movements", "loss of consciousness", "staring spells", "stiff muscles"], "other_symptoms": ["short term confusion", "sudden fear or anxiety", "deja vu sensation", "aura before seizure", "temporary unresponsiveness", "repetitive movements", "hallucinations", "dizziness"], "red_flags": ["seizure lasting more than five minutes", "repeated seizures without recovery", "breathing does not return after seizure", "first time seizure", "injury during seizure", "seizure in person with diabetes or pregnancy"], "risk_factors": ["prior brain injury", "brain infection", "stroke history", "brain tumor", "genetic predisposition", "developmental disorders", "family history of epilepsy"], "actions": ["protect the person from injury", "place the person on their side after convulsions stop", "do not restrain movements", "do not place anything in the mouth", "call emergency services if seizure lasts more than five minutes"], "triage_priority": "Urgent (Within 1 Hour)", "common_tests": ["electroencephalogram", "brain MRI", "brain CT scan", "blood tests"], "source": "Mayo Clinic"}

{"id": "CARD_MI_002", "disease_name": "Heart Attack (Myocardial Infarction)", "category": "Cardiovascular", "severity": "Highly Critical", "description": "A heart attack occurs when blood flow to part of the heart muscle is severely reduced or blocked, usually due to plaque buildup and clot formation in the coronary arteries.", "key_symptoms": ["chest pain", "chest pressure", "chest tightness", "shortness of breath", "pain spreading to arm", "pain spreading to jaw", "pain spreading to neck"], "other_symptoms": ["cold sweat", "fatigue", "nausea", "lightheadedness", "dizziness", "heartburn", "indigestion", "back pain", "upper abdominal discomfort"], "red_flags": ["persistent chest pain lasting more than a few minutes", "chest pain that spreads to arm jaw or back", "sudden shortness of breath with chest discomfort", "loss of consciousness", "suspected heart attack symptoms"], "risk_factors": ["coronary artery disease", "smoking", "high blood pressure", "high cholesterol", "obesity", "diabetes", "metabolic syndrome", "family history of heart attack", "physical inactivity", "unhealthy diet", "chronic stress", "illicit drug use", "older age", "male sex", "history of preeclampsia", "autoimmune disease"], "actions": ["call emergency services immediately", "chew aspirin if recommended and not allergic", "take prescribed nitroglycerin if available", "keep the person calm and seated", "start CPR if the person becomes unconscious and not breathing"], "triage_priority": "Very High Risk (within 10-30 Minutes)", "common_tests": ["electrocardiogram", "troponin blood test", "coronary angiography", "cardiac CT scan"], "source":
"Mayo Clinic"}

{"id": "EMG_HEP_002", "disease_name": "Hepatic Encephalopathy", "category": "Liver", "severity": "Critical", "description": "A serious brain dysfunction caused by the liver's inability to remove toxins such as ammonia from the blood, leading to confusion, altered consciousness, and potentially coma.", "key_symptoms": ["confusion", "difficulty concentrating", "memory problems", "daytime sleepiness", "personality changes", "slurred speech", "flapping tremor"], "red_flags": ["severe confusion", "inability to wake", "coma", "marked behavior changes", "rapid mental decline"], "common_causes": ["cirrhosis", "acute liver failure", "gastrointestinal bleeding", "infection", "electrolyte imbalance", "constipation", "dehydration", "sedative medications"], "risk_factors": ["advanced liver disease", "alcohol use", "older age", "diabetes", "kidney disease", "low sodium levels", "high ammonia levels", "history of hepatic encephalopathy"], "triage_priority": "Urgent", "actions": ["assess airway breathing circulation", "check mental status", "measure ammonia and electrolytes", "identify precipitating cause", "start lactulose if indicated", "prepare for hospital admission"], "complications": ["coma", "falls and injuries", "permanent cognitive impairment", "recurrent hospitalizations", "death"], "keywords": ["hepatic encephalopathy", "ammonia brain", "cirrhosis confusion", "liver failure confusion", "flapping tremor confusion"]}

{"id": "EMG_END_002", "disease_name": "Hypoglycemia", "category": "Endocrine", "severity": "Highly Critical", "description": "Hypoglycemia is a condition in which blood glucose levels fall below the normal range, depriving the body and brain of their primary energy source and potentially causing severe neurological symptoms.", "key_symptoms": ["pale skin", "shakiness", "sweating", "headache", "hunger or nausea", "rapid or irregular heartbeat", "fatigue", "irritability or anxiety", "difficulty concentrating", "dizziness or lightheadedness", "tingling of lips or tongue", "confusion", "blurred vision", "loss of coordination", "slurred speech"], "red_flags": ["loss of consciousness", "seizures", "severe confusion", "unresponsiveness", "hypoglycemia not improving after sugar intake"], "common_causes": ["excess insulin", "diabetes medications", "skipping meals", "excessive exercise", "heavy alcohol intake without food", "severe liver disease", "kidney disease", "severe infection", "insulinoma", "hormone deficiencies", "malnutrition or starvation", "post-bariatric surgery reactive hypoglycemia"], "risk_factors": ["diabetes mellitus", "insulin therapy", "oral hypoglycemic drugs", "chronic kidney disease", "chronic liver disease", "alcohol misuse", "advanced age", "history of recurrent hypoglycemia", "hypoglycemia unawareness"], "triage_priority": "Very High Risk (within 10-30 Minutes)", "actions": ["check blood glucose immediately", "administer fast-acting carbohydrates if conscious", "give IV dextrose if unconscious", "administer glucagon if IV access unavailable", "monitor airway and vital signs", "identify and treat underlying cause"], "complications": ["seizures", "coma", "brain injury", "falls and trauma", "death", "hypoglycemia unawareness"], "noise_keywords_to_ignore": ["mild hunger", "routine fatigue", "dieting without
symptoms", "old resolved low sugar episode"], "source": "Mayo Clinic"}

{"id": "EMG_NEU_001", "disease_name": "Meningitis", "category": "Neurological", "severity": "Critical", "description": "Inflammation of the meninges around the brain and spinal cord, most often caused by infection. Requires urgent evaluation.", "key_symptoms": ["sudden high fever", "stiff neck", "severe headache", "nausea or vomiting", "confusion", "seizures", "sleepiness or difficulty waking", "sensitivity to light", "skin rash (sometimes)"], "red_flags": ["stiff neck with fever", "confusion or altered consciousness", "seizures", "persistent vomiting", "difficulty waking", "bulging fontanel in infants"], "common_causes": ["viral infection", "bacterial infection", "fungal infection", "parasitic infection"], "risk_factors": ["lack of vaccination", "age under 5 or under 20", "weakened immune system", "living in crowded settings", "pregnancy"], "triage_priority": "Urgent (Within 1 Hour)", "actions": ["treat as medical emergency", "initiate urgent medical evaluation", "start empiric antibiotics if bacterial suspected", "isolate if meningococcal suspected", "monitor airway and consciousness"], "complications": ["brain damage", "hearing loss", "seizures", "shock", "death"], "keywords": ["meningitis", "stiff neck fever", "brain infection", "meningeal inflammation", "photophobia with fever"]}

{"id": "EMG_RESP_001", "disease_name": "Pneumothorax", "category": "Respiratory", "severity": "Critical", "description": "Collapsed lung caused by air entering the space between the lung and chest wall, leading to lung compression and breathing difficulty.", "key_symptoms": ["sudden chest pain", "shortness of breath", "rapid breathing", "sharp chest pain with breathing", "fatigue"], "red_flags": ["severe shortness of breath", "worsening chest pain", "cyanosis", "rapid heart rate", "respiratory distress"], "common_causes": ["chest injury", "underlying lung disease", "ruptured lung blebs", "mechanical ventilation", "spontaneous occurrence"], "risk_factors": ["smoking", "male sex", "tall thin body type", "previous pneumothorax", "chronic lung disease", "genetic predisposition"], "triage_priority": "Immediate", "actions": ["assess airway and breathing immediately", "administer oxygen", "prepare for needle decompression if tension suspected", "arrange urgent chest imaging", "insert chest tube if indicated"], "complications": ["tension pneumothorax", "respiratory failure", "recurrent pneumothorax", "cardiovascular collapse"], "keywords": ["pneumothorax", "collapsed lung", "sudden chest pain breathlessness", "air in pleural space", "lung collapse"]}

{"id": "EMG_CARD_003", "disease_name": "Pulmonary Embolism", "category": "Respiratory", "severity": "Critical", "description": "A pulmonary embolism is a blockage in a lung artery usually caused by a blood clot that travels from the leg (DVT). It can be life-threatening without prompt treatment.", "key_symptoms": ["sudden shortness of breath", "sharp chest pain worse with breathing", "fainting (syncope)", "rapid or irregular heartbeat", "cough with bloody mucus", "leg pain or swelling", "dizziness", "excessive sweating", "cyanosis"], "red_flags": ["unexplained shortness of breath", "chest pain resembling heart attack", "fainting", "bloody cough", "signs of DVT (leg swelling/pain)"], "common_causes": ["deep vein thrombosis (DVT)", "prolonged immobility", "recent surgery", "fat embolism", "air embolism", "tumor fragments"], "risk_factors": ["history of blood clots", "heart disease", "cancer", "recent surgery", "clotting disorders", "COVID-19 infection", "prolonged bed rest", "long-distance travel", "smoking", "obesity", "estrogen therapy", "pregnancy"], "triage_priority": "Immediate", "actions": ["seek emergency medical care immediately", "administer oxygen if available", "monitor vital signs", "prepare for anticoagulation therapy per protocol"], "complications": ["pulmonary hypertension", "lung tissue damage (pulmonary infarction)", "right heart strain", "death if untreated"], "noise_keywords_to_ignore": ["dental history", "old minor injuries", "non-related chronic conditions"], "source": "Mayo Clinic"}

{"id": "EMG_MED_001", "disease_name": "Sepsis", "category": "Infectious", "severity": "Serious", "description": "Sepsis is a life-threatening condition caused by the body's extreme response to an infection, leading to organ dysfunction and possible septic shock.", "key_symptoms": ["altered mental status", "fast shallow breathing", "unexplained sweating", "lightheadedness", "shivering", "extreme confusion", "difficulty staying awake"], "red_flags": ["confusion or altered consciousness", "rapid breathing", "very low blood pressure", "extreme drowsiness", "signs of infection not improving"], "common_causes": ["bacterial infections", "viral infections", "fungal infections", "pneumonia", "urinary tract infection", "abdominal infection", "bloodstream infection", "infected wounds or burns", "catheter-related infection"], "risk_factors": ["age over 65", "infancy", "weakened immune system", "cancer treatment", "HIV infection", "diabetes", "chronic kidney disease", "COPD", "recent ICU stay", "indwelling catheters", "recent antibiotic use", "corticosteroid therapy"], "triage_priority": "Very High Risk (within 10-30 Minutes)", "actions": ["activate emergency response", "administer oxygen", "start IV access", "begin broad-spectrum antibiotics per protocol", "monitor blood pressure and urine output", "prepare for rapid fluid resuscitation"], "complications": ["septic shock", "multi-organ failure", "abnormal blood clotting", "tissue damage", "death"], "noise_keywords_to_ignore": ["old dental procedures", "minor past infections", "resolved childhood illnesses"], "source": "Mayo Clinic"}

{"id": "NEU_STROKE_003", "disease_name": "Stroke", "category": "Neurological", "severity": "Highly Critical", "description": "A stroke occurs when blood supply to part of the brain is blocked or when a blood vessel in the brain ruptures, leading to brain cell death within minutes.", "key_symptoms": ["sudden trouble speaking", "slurred speech", "confusion", "face drooping", "arm weakness", "leg weakness", "one sided numbness", "sudden vision problems", "sudden severe headache", "trouble walking", "loss of balance", "loss of coordination"], "other_symptoms": ["blurred vision", "double vision", "dizziness", "vomiting", "change in consciousness", "fainting"], "red_flags": ["face drooping on one side", "arm weakness or drift", "slurred or abnormal speech", "sudden confusion with neurological deficit", "sudden severe headache with neurological symptoms", "sudden loss of vision", "suspected FAST positive stroke signs"], "risk_factors": ["high blood pressure", "smoking", "high cholesterol", "diabetes", "obesity", "physical inactivity", "heavy alcohol use", "illicit drug use", "cardiovascular disease", "atrial fibrillation", "obstructive sleep apnea", "family history of stroke", "previous stroke or transient ischemic attack", "older age", "male sex"], "actions": ["call emergency services immediately", "note the time symptoms started", "keep the person safe and lying on their side if unconscious", "do not give food or drink", "monitor breathing and start CPR if unresponsive and not breathing"], "triage_priority": "Immediate", "common_tests": ["CT scan of the brain", "MRI of the
brain", "electrocardiogram", "carotid ultrasound", "blood tests"], "source": "Mayo Clinic"}

{"id": "EMG_CARD_004", "disease_name": "Ventricular Tachycardia", "category": "Cardiovascular", "severity": "Fatal", "description": "Ventricular tachycardia is a fast, abnormal heart rhythm originating in the ventricles that can reduce cardiac output and may lead to sudden cardiac arrest if sustained.", "key_symptoms": ["rapid heartbeat", "palpitations", "chest pain", "shortness of breath", "dizziness", "lightheadedness"], "red_flags": ["fainting", "loss of consciousness", "sustained rapid heart rate", "cardiac arrest", "chest pain lasting more than a few minutes", "severe breathing difficulty"], "common_causes": ["prior heart attack", "structural heart disease", "coronary artery disease", "long QT syndrome", "electrolyte imbalance", "medication side effects", "stimulant drug use", "idiopathic causes"], "risk_factors": ["heart disease", "history of myocardial infarction", "electrolyte abnormalities", "family history of arrhythmia", "stimulant drug use", "certain cardiac medications"], "triage_priority": "Immediate", "actions": ["assess airway, breathing, and circulation", "obtain ECG immediately", "provide oxygen if hypoxic", "prepare for synchronized cardioversion if unstable", "administer antiarrhythmic medications as indicated", "activate emergency cardiac response"], "complications": ["ventricular fibrillation", "sudden cardiac arrest", "heart failure", "recurrent syncope", "sudden death"], "noise_keywords_to_ignore": ["brief anxiety-related palpitations", "isolated premature beats", "remote history of mild tachycardia"], "source": "Mayo Clinic"}
